topics.title: AI Announcements
topics.executive_summary: Microsoft launched its first proprietary AI models—MAI-Code-1-Flash for coding and MAI-Thinking-1 for reasoning—at Build 2026, while Meta began monetizing its AI Business Agent, signaling Big Tech's race to build homegrown alternatives to OpenAI and Anthropic.

stories.headline: Microsoft debuts proprietary AI models at Build 2026
stories.body: <cite index="1-8">Microsoft announced MAI-Code-1-Flash, its inaugural model that takes written descriptions from people and spits out source code for applications and websites.</cite> <cite index="1-10,1-11">In addition to MAI-Code-1-Flash, Microsoft is introducing MAI-Thinking-1, a reasoning model, built for high efficiency and performance, but importantly, at a low-token cost.</cite>

stories.headline: Microsoft unveils Copilot super app with new agents
stories.body: <cite index="5-1">Microsoft will be unveiling a Copilot super app this summer that will combine chat, coding, and a function named Autopilot.</cite> <cite index="5-9">Autopilot is designed to connect to an agent named Scout, the first of a new category of agents that Nadella said will be able to join group chats in Microsoft Teams or handle email threads in Outlook.</cite>

stories.headline: Meta monetizes AI Business Agent for companies
stories.body: <cite index="3-15,3-16">Meta will soon begin selling access to an AI agent in a new monetization path for the tech giant increasingly pushing into AI. As of Wednesday, companies will now be charged for access to the "Meta Business Agent."</cite> <cite index="3-17">The agent's primary role focuses on communication with a business's customers on Meta's communications platforms WhatsApp, Messenger, and Instagram.</cite>

topics.title: Startups
topics.executive_summary: No major startup funding news was published in the last 24 hours that could be confirmed through search results, suggesting a quiet day in the sector.

topics.title: AI Infrastructure
topics.executive_summary: Digi Power X committed $35 million to NVIDIA's next-generation Vera Rubin GPU platform, betting on advanced AI infrastructure for its GPU-as-a-Service business with deployment starting Q1 2027.

stories.headline: Digi Power X Bets $35M on NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPUs
stories.body: <cite index="4-1">Digi Power X announced a $35 million commitment to purchase NVIDIA's next-generation Vera Rubin platform to expand its NeoCloudz GPU-as-a-Service business</cite>. <cite index="4-2">The Vera Rubin systems pair Rubin GPUs (288GB HBM4) with the 88-core Vera CPU over NVLink 6</cite>, representing NVIDIA's rack-scale successor to Blackwell.

topics.title: Deep Tech
topics.executive_summary: No major deep tech news was published in the last 24 hours that could be confirmed through search results, suggesting a quiet day in the sector.
